sourceMovie.title: Mississippi Burning
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mississippi Burning works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Wind River
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mississippi Burning clicked for you, Wind River (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mississippi Burning?
faq.answer: Try Wind River, In the Heat of the Night, Gaslight—each shares DNA with Mississippi Burning while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mississippi Burning?
faq.answer: Mississippi Burning (1988) is best described as Drama, Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mississippi Burning worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
